commit.message:
Add JsonPatch.EnumerateArray API with propagator append merge (#56826)

* Add JsonPatch.EnumerateArray API with propagator append merge

Add EnumerateArray method to JsonPatch that iterates over JSON array
elements at a given path, returning each as ReadOnlyMemory<byte>.

- JsonPatch.Iterate.cs: EnumerateArray, ResolveArray, ArrayEnumerator
- JsonPatch.Implementation.cs: merge ArrayItemAppend with propagator
  results in TryGetEncodedValueInternal so GetJson stays consistent
- AvailabilitySetDataV2: test model with enhanced PropagateGet that
  serializes CLR collections via ModelReaderWriter.Write for
  no-round-trip array enumeration
- 61 tests across 7 categories covering seed-only, properties-only,
  hybrid, propagator-backed (V1 + V2), error cases, and edge cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update API listings for EnumerateArray

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove redundant array validation byte check in EnumerateArray

The firstByte check was redundant with the ArrayEnumerator constructor
which validates via Utf8JsonReader. The byte check also couldn't handle
leading whitespace in raw JSON bytes for root-path arrays.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Respect Removed state in ResolveArray root fallback

When TryGetEncodedValueInternal returns a Removed value, throw
KeyNotFoundException instead of falling back to seed bytes. This
makes EnumerateArray consistent with other JsonPatch getters.

Added tests for removed root/nested arrays and root non-array types.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use computed format in exception messages instead of options.Format

Fix both read and write paths to use the resolved format variable
instead of options.Format, which could misleadingly show 'W'.

fix(llm): force MiniMax tool_choice required/specific to fix tc=0

MiniMax M2.5 ignores tool_choice='auto' and never calls tools.
Fix by forcing:
- 'required' when multiple tools available (must call at least one)
- {'type':'function','function':{'name':X}} when only one tool available
  (happens after write_only_tools strip in executor)

This is the root cause of tc=0 at all rounds for MiniMax agents.
Combined with the existing tool-result visibility fix (role=tool→user),
agents can now: call tools → see results → call write tools.

commit.message:
Add --since-last-time option to recent-scrobbles (#29)

## Summary

- Add `--since-last-time` flag to `spfm lastfm recent-scrobbles` to
automatically fetch only new scrobbles since the last run
- Track scrobble count persistently in `~/.spotfm/lastfm_state.json`
(owned 100% by spotfm)
- Every run (with or without the flag) updates the state file

## Motivation

Previously, getting new scrobbles required a manual workflow:
1. Check `data/spotify-2026.yaml` for last count
2. Check Last.FM UI for current count
3. Calculate diff manually
4. Run with `-l <diff>`

Now it's just: `spfm lastfm recent-scrobbles --since-last-time`

## Usage

```bash
# First run — initializes state file (use -l as usual)
spfm lastfm recent-scrobbles -l 38

# Subsequent runs — fully automated
spfm lastfm recent-scrobbles --since-last-time
# → Fetching 38 new scrobbles (was 135, now 173).
# → Artist - Track - 5 - 10 - https://...
```

## State file format

`~/.spotfm/lastfm_state.json`:
```json
{
  "last_scrobble_count": 173,
  "last_run_date": "2026-03-06"
}
```

## Edge cases

- No state file yet → prints helpful message: "No previous state found.
Run once without --since-last-time to initialize."
- No new scrobbles → prints "No new scrobbles since last run." and
updates state

## Test plan

- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/test_lastfm.py tests/test_utils.py` — 83
tests pass
- [ ] `make test` — 262 tests pass
- [ ] `spfm lastfm recent-scrobbles -l 5` — runs and creates
`~/.spotfm/lastfm_state.json`
- [ ] `spfm lastfm recent-scrobbles --since-last-time` — fetches diff
automatically

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix(blazor): fix circuit startup - use correct blazor.web.js API format

The Blazor.start() configuration was using the old blazor.server.js API format
(top-level reconnectionOptions/reconnectionHandler/dialogId), but the app uses
blazor.web.js which requires these options nested under 'circuit: { ... }'.

This caused the Blazor interactive circuit to fail to establish, resulting in:
- Tabs not responding to clicks (no @onclick handling)
- Buttons not functioning (no circuit = no event handlers)
- Accordions not expanding/collapsing (no @onclick)
- Navigation falling back to enhanced navigation (page-level)

Changes:
- Move reconnectionOptions/reconnectionHandler under circuit property
- Remove unsupported dialogId option (blazor.server.js only)
- Remove Blazor.reconnect override (blazor.server.js API, not blazor.web.js)
- Use retryIntervalMilliseconds as function for exponential backoff
- Add .catch() for circuit startup error handling
- Increment service worker cache version (v3 -> v4) to bust stale caches

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add two new commands that fetch images from the local machine through
the existing SSH tunnel:

- `gh rdm screenshot` — grabs the latest Screenshot*.png from ~/Desktop
- `gh rdm clipboard-image` — grabs the current PNG from the macOS clipboard

Reduce peak memory in blas_level_3 tests (#1031)

* Split blas_level_3 tests into 3a-3d to reduce peak memory

hand_written test cases are split across four Val symbols to keep peak
memory at ~8-10 MB per set vs ~33 MB if run as one block. Array refs in
test-case tuples stay live for the duration of each @testset loop, so
running sets sequentially lets GC reclaim each before the next is built.

- gemm! mat×mat (dominant cost) split by tB across 3a/3b/3c
- mat×vec and tB='N' mat×mat in 3a
- tB='T' mat×mat in 3b
- tB='C' mat×mat and vec×vec in 3c
- vec×
  called via run_derived_rule_test_cases directly to avoid a dummy
  hand_written companion

* Replace scalar Cartesian product with 3 representative tuples in blas_level_3a-3f

The old code took the full Cartesian product of αs × dαs × βs × dβs
(3×3×3×3 = 81 combos) for each outer (tA, tB, P) combo.  The gemm!
derivative is linear in all four scalars, so this gives no extra
coverage: if the formula is correct for one set of values it is correct
for all.  The three cases that actually matter are:

  - β = 0            (real scalars, exercises the β=0 branch)
  - β ≠ 0, real      (real scalars, full formula)
  - complex scalars  (filtered to ComplexF32/ComplexF64)

Replacing the product with these three explicit (α, dα, β, dβ) tuples
reduces the test-case count by ~20× across 3a–3f, fixing the
blas_level_3-1.12-x64 CI timeout.

commit.message:
fix(wasm): remove ASYNCIFY linker flags causing SHELL:-s link error

The ASYNCIFY -s flags were:
1. Breaking the linker: 'QMAKE_LFLAGS += -s ASYNCIFY=1' causes qmake to
   emit 'SHELL:-s' literally into the Makefile link command, which em++
   interprets as a filename rather than a flag (error: SHELL:-s: No such
   file or directory).
2. Unnecessary: the WebBluetooth bridge uses EM_JS with JavaScript
   async/await internally. C++ never blocks - all BLE results arrive
   via the bleNotifyC() EMSCRIPTEN_KEEPALIVE callback. No ASYNCIFY
   instrumentation is needed on the C++ side.

commit.message:
fix(ci): harden docs build and stable-docs commit

- ci.yml: add 'Ensure Stable API Docs Are Available' guard step before
  mkdocs build. If docs/api/stable/ has no package subdirectories, replace
  index.md with a valid placeholder so strict-mode mkdocs build succeeds.
  Fixes the immediate CI failure caused by broken links in stable/index.md.

- ci.yml: replace git-stash in 'Commit Coverage Badge' with cp-to-temp.
  The stash approach is fragile; saving to a temp file then pulling before
  restoring is deterministic and handles concurrent pushes correctly.

- release.yml: replace git-stash in 'Commit Stable API Docs to Main' with
  cp-to-temp. git stash only stashes tracked-file changes -- newly-generated
  package subdirectories are untracked and silently dropped. Using cp before
  the branch switch captures everything on disk regardless of git state.
  This is the root-cause fix: stable package subdirs will now be committed
  to main after each release.

- scripts/validate-docs.sh: new local validation script. Checks stable and
  dev index link integrity and runs mkdocs build --strict. Run before pushing
  to catch doc build failures early.

commit.message:
fix: dashboard 401 expired JWT — skip verify_exp for EasyAuth tokens

Root cause: pyjwt.decode() with verify_exp=True rejects EasyAuth-injected
JWTs whose exp has passed, even though EasyAuth already validated the
session. ExpiredSignatureError was never caught (sibling of DecodeError,
not a subclass), falling through to the generic handler.

Fix: detect EasyAuth-sourced tokens (X-MS-TOKEN-AAD-ID-TOKEN header
present) and skip exp verification for them. Direct Bearer tokens
(API/Swagger) still get exp checked.

Also includes frontend improvements from parallel session:
- api.ts: clear stale cached token before retry, rebuild headers
- Dashboard.tsx: session-expired UI with re-login button

run autonomously with no interactive user. Without this, the SDK defaults
to requesting user permission via JSON-RPC which fails with 'Permission

approval via permission.request RPC calls. Without this handler, the SDK
cannot get user consent when running autonomously and returns 'Permission
denied and could not request permission from user' for all tool calls
including the Exit tool.

commit.message:
fix: only show registered marketplaces, simplify remove to CLI-only (#116)

## Summary

Cache-only marketplace directories (cloned by the CLI as a side-effect
of browsing/installing, but never explicitly registered) are now
ignored. Only marketplaces the user explicitly added via \copilot plugin
marketplace add\ are shown.

### Changes
- **\listMarketplaces()\**: Only returns entries from \config.json\
(registered). Cache-only dirs are intentionally ignored — they're CLI
implementation details, not user-configured marketplaces.
- **\ emoveMarketplace()\**: Simplified to a single CLI command
(\copilot plugin marketplace remove <key>\). No more dual-path \
s.rmSync\ fallback for cache-only entries.
- **\server.mjs\ / \server-prod.mjs\**: Routes updated to match new
signatures.
- **\pluginService.ts\ / \PluginHub.tsx\**: Delete button is now visible
for all non-protected entries (all listed entries always have a \
egisteredKey\). Removed the \m.registeredKey\ guard that was hiding the
button.
- Added \marketplaceService.d.mts\ for TypeScript types.
- Removed stale 'registeredKey is null' regression test; added test
verifying cache-only dirs are NOT listed.

### Why cache-only entries were confusing
The CLI downloads marketplace repos to \~/.copilot/marketplace-cache/\
as a side-effect of browsing, but doesn't register them in
\config.json\. Showing these entries was misleading because they weren't
something the user consciously added, and trying to delete them via
\copilot plugin marketplace remove\ would fail since they're not
registered.

## Tests
- 22 marketplace tests pass (14 service + 8 UI)

microtask and flush them all in one call — one redraw per render.

commit.message:
fix(shared): atomic rate limiter increment — eliminate TOCTOU race (H-18)

Added `increment(key, resetTime)` to RateLimitStore interface that
atomically reads, increments, and returns the new count in a single
synchronous Map mutation (no await gap between read and write).

Rewrote recordFailedAttempt() to use the atomic increment instead of
the previous get() → count++ → set() pattern that allowed concurrent
requests to both read count=4 and bypass MAX_ATTEMPTS=5.

Updated Redis example in JSDoc to show INCR-based implementation.
